items.back: At the time the approach clearance is issued.

items.front: STAR/IAP connection: minimum distance to issue clearance
items.back: At least 3 NM prior to the IAF/IF.

items.front: Radar vector to final: max intercept angle

items.back: Only when FAF is also an IAF; aircraft expected to execute procedure turn/hold-in-lieu.

items.front: Intercept <= 90 degrees with procedure turn: straight-in required
items.back: Instruct 'CLEARED STRAIGHT-IN (type) APPROACH' if ATC does not want procedure turn.
Must not disapprove a pilot request to cold temperature compensate with approach clearance.
Only after established on published route/IAP segment or assigned altitude until established on segment.
Only for airports with operational control towers.
Eight cardinal compass points and left/right base/downwind leg as appropriate.
Approach to one runway, landing on a parallel runway. Requires higher weather minima/MDA.
Approach clearance automatically authorizes missed approach for IFR aircraft (not VFR practice).

items.front: Alternate missed approach via NOTAM: description requirement
items.back: Need not be issued to pilot if published and in effect via NOTAM.
When pilot states unfamiliarity with the procedure. Includes initial altitude, procedure turn, final approach course, and missed approach.

items.front: VFR practice approach: initial contact instruction
items.back: Must be instructed to maintain VFR.

items.front: VFR practice without IFR separation: phraseology
items.back: MAINTAIN VFR, PRACTICE APPROACH APPROVED, NO SEPARATION SERVICES PROVIDED.
NOT automatic; must be specifically requested by pilot and approved by controller.

items.front: Touch-and-go / low approach: arrival-to-departure transition point
items.back: When aircraft touches down or crosses landing threshold.

items.front: Low approach / touch-and-go: when to issue departure instructions
items.back: Before final descent. Include heading/route and altitude (except VFR to tower).
